scenario: Reopen button disabled when reason has fewer than 5 characters
whyItMatters: Audit log entries must be meaningful — blank reasons create audit gaps.
risk: Allowing blank reason bypasses the audit requirement.

story: calls onEnable with reason and duration on submit
scenario: providing valid reason and duration enables Reopen button and calls onEnable
whyItMatters: Reopen action must pass correct reason and minutes to the audit RPC.
risk: Wrong duration (e.g. hours not converted) gives longer access than intended.

story: listProjects maps project_no to group_no and advisor_name to advisor
scenario: listProjects normalizes DB row shape for UI consumption
whyItMatters: UI code reads group_no and advisor — wrong mapping silently shows blank fields.
risk: Missing map means group numbers and advisor fields appear empty for every project.

story: createProject inserts and returns the created row
scenario: createProject resolves with the DB-returned row on success
whyItMatters: Edit drawer saves via createProject and immediately displays the returned data.
risk: Returning undefined would cause the new row to not appear until refresh.

scenario: deleteProject resolves undefined when delete succeeds
whyItMatters: Caller checks for thrown errors to determine success.
risk: Silently swallowing the error would keep the row in the UI while deleted in DB.

scenario: returns raw array from DB for the given period
whyItMatters: Outcome mapping table is built entirely from this list.
risk: Empty return would show no mappings even when they exist.

story: createPeriodOutcome calls correct RPC with payload
scenario: rpc_admin_create_period_outcome called with p_code from payload
whyItMatters: Outcome creation must reach the correct RPC or silently fail.
risk: Wrong RPC name means outcomes are never saved and the user sees no error.

scenario: rpc_admin_delete_period_outcome called with correct outcome id
whyItMatters: Delete must reach the correct RPC or silently no-op.
risk: Wrong id parameter would delete wrong outcome or nothing.

story: listOrganizations maps RPC rows to UI shape
scenario: returns organizations with shortLabel, tenantAdmins, pendingApplications
whyItMatters: Platform Control page builds the org list from this shape.
risk: Missing key silently renders empty org panels.

scenario: throws when supabase returns an error object
whyItMatters: Error must surface to the UI; silent swallow leaves stale data.
risk: Catching the error without re-throwing would show empty list as success.

story: createOrganization inserts and returns new org row
scenario: createOrganization resolves with the DB-returned row
whyItMatters: Super-admin org creation must return the new row so UI can update immediately.
risk: Returning undefined causes super-admin to think creation failed.

story: buildOutcomes maps criteria to display shape
scenario: returns array of {id, key, label, max, rubric, code} from criteria array
whyItMatters: All analytics charts depend on this shape to render outcome columns.
risk: Wrong key name silently produces undefined in all chart cells.

scenario: returns empty array when criteria is null or undefined
whyItMatters: Analytics tab renders before data loads; must not throw.
risk: Uncaught TypeError crashes the analytics tab for orgs with no criteria.

story: getCriterionColor finds color by criterion id
scenario: returns color string when a criterion with matching id exists
whyItMatters: Chart series colors must match the configured criterion color.
risk: Color mismatch makes charts inconsistent with the criteria editor.

story: getCriterionColor returns fallback when id not found
scenario: returns fallback value when no criterion matches the given id
whyItMatters: Charts must not render colorless series when a criterion is deleted.
risk: undefined color causes chart rendering errors in production.

story: formatOutcomeCodes splits and joins outcome codes
scenario: PO1/PO2 becomes 'PO1 / PO2' with trimmed segments
whyItMatters: Outcome code display in charts and tables depends on this formatter.
risk: Un-trimmed or missing spaces break the display label.

scenario: returns empty string for null, undefined, or empty code
whyItMatters: Criteria with no outcomes must render a blank code, not 'null'.
risk: Displaying 'null' or 'undefined' in chart labels confuses users.

story: outcomeCodeLine wraps formatted code in parentheses
scenario: returns '(PO1 / PO2)' for a slash-joined code string
whyItMatters: Chart tooltip and label display use the parenthesized form.
risk: Missing parens or wrong format breaks label readability.

story: outcomeCodeLine returns empty string for empty code
scenario: returns '' when code is empty or null
whyItMatters: Criteria with no outcomes must produce no label suffix.
risk: Empty parens '()' in chart labels look like a bug.

scenario: returns formatted % avg across all criteria and all submission rows
whyItMatters: KPI strip 'Overall Average' card uses this value.
risk: Wrong normalization (raw instead of %) makes the KPI card meaningless.

story: computeOverallAvg returns null for empty data
scenario: returns null when submittedData is empty or null
whyItMatters: KPI card must show '--' not '0%' when no submissions exist.
risk: Returning 0 implies a real score, misleading admins.

story: buildAttainmentStatusDataset returns Met/Borderline/Not Met status rows
scenario: attainment >= threshold → Met; >= 60 → Borderline; < 60 → Not Met
whyItMatters: Attainment Status sheet drives MÜDEK accreditation reporting.
risk: Wrong thresholds mis-classify outcomes, invalidating accreditation reports.

story: buildAttainmentStatusDataset with empty data returns sheet structure
scenario: returns sheet object with empty rows and zero summary counts
whyItMatters: Export must still produce a valid sheet even when no data exists.
risk: Null or missing sheet object crashes the Excel export builder.

scenario: returns rows with avgPct and gapPct computed from submission values
whyItMatters: Threshold Gap sheet shows which outcomes need intervention.
risk: Wrong gap sign or unit (raw vs %) makes the sheet misleading.

story: buildGroupHeatmapDataset with empty groups returns empty rows
scenario: returns sheet object with empty rows array when no groups have submissions
whyItMatters: Heatmap must render gracefully before any group has been scored.
risk: Undefined rows array crashes the heatmap renderer on first load.

story: buildAttainmentRateDataset computes per-outcome attainment rate (% ≥ threshold)
scenario: returns rows with pct reflecting share of evaluations scoring ≥ threshold per mapped criterion
whyItMatters: Attainment Rate export must match the on-screen chart; previously exported Programme Averages data under the wrong label.
risk: Users and accreditors see inconsistent numbers between the chart and the exported Excel/PDF.

story: buildAttainmentStatusDataset uses outcome-level prior stats for delta column
scenario: when priorPeriodStats has {currentTrend,prevTrend} with outcomes[], delta is current.avg − prev.avg per outcome code
whyItMatters: Delta column must compare the same outcome across periods; criterion-level deltas collapse two outcomes sharing a criterion into one number.
risk: Stakeholders misread period-over-period change when two outcomes share a criterion but diverge in reality.

story: buildOutcomeAttainmentTrendExportDataset flattens trend data into period × outcome table
scenario: returns headers Period/N plus att% and avg% per outcome code, rows ordered chronologically
whyItMatters: Trend export previously used the wrong source (criterion-level avgTechnical/… columns) and lost the per-outcome attainment rate.
risk: Trend sheet shows no attainment percentages and mislabels fixed criterion columns as outcomes.

story: buildCoverageMatrixDataset produces outcome×criterion matrix with Direct/Indirect/—
scenario: returns one row per outcome with cells per criterion classified via outcomeTypes; summary extra table counts mapping types
whyItMatters: Coverage Matrix export must mirror the on-screen matrix; the previous flat criteria→outcome list lost the Indirect distinction.
risk: Accreditation reviewers cannot verify direct vs indirect assessment coverage from the exported Excel/PDF.

story: buildGroupHeatmapDataset produces group rows × criterion columns with cells-below-threshold tail column
scenario: returns headers [Group, ...criteria labels, Cells Below Threshold] and one row per project group with P<n> — title label
whyItMatters: Export must stay compact and readable; long project titles wrap when used as column headers and criterion labels are short, so groups belong on rows.
risk: Wrapped multi-line column headers or unreadable columns force reviewers to widen PDF pages or reconcile multiple orientations.

story: compareOutcomeCodes orders PO codes naturally (PO 2 before PO 10.1)
scenario: Array.sort(compareOutcomeCodes) yields PO 1.1, PO 1.2, PO 1.10, PO 2, PO 3.1, PO 10.1, PO 10.2, PO 11
whyItMatters: Outcome trend heatmap and trend export list codes in programme order; default lexicographic sort misplaces two-digit codes and breaks reviewer expectations.
risk: Heatmap rows appear in a non-intuitive order, making outcomes hard to locate in accreditation reports.

story: nextCriterionColor picks first unused color from palette
scenario: skips colors already used by existing rows and returns first unused one
whyItMatters: Criteria editor auto-assigns distinct colors; collisions make criteria indistinguishable.
risk: Duplicate colors make criteria rows look the same in heatmaps and charts.

story: nextCriterionColor wraps around when all palette colors are used
scenario: falls back to palette[length % palette.length] when all colors are taken
whyItMatters: Projects with >8 criteria must not crash or return undefined.
risk: undefined color passed to getCriterionTintStyle returns a broken style.

story: defaultRubricBands generates 4 bands with correct level names
scenario: returns [Excellent, Good, Developing, Insufficient] bands scaled to max
whyItMatters: New criteria start with sensible rubric bands so admins don't have to build from scratch.
risk: Wrong band names or wrong order breaks the rubric editor display.

story: defaultRubricBands uses default max=30 for invalid input
scenario: returns bands scaled to 30 when max is null, undefined, or 0
whyItMatters: Criteria with unset max must still get a valid rubric seed.
risk: NaN in band min/max causes the rubric editor to show empty fields.

story: getConfigRubricSeed matches criterion by key and returns rubric copy
scenario: finds criterion by _key, returns shallow copy of its rubric bands
whyItMatters: Copy-from-config action in the criteria editor depends on this lookup.
risk: Returning the original reference allows mutations to corrupt the source criterion.

story: getConfigRubricSeed returns null when no criterion matches
scenario: returns null when row key does not match any criterion in the list
whyItMatters: Copy-from-config must gracefully no-op when no match exists.
risk: Returning an empty array instead of null skips the defaultRubricBands fallback.

story: clampToCriterionMax clamps value above max to max
scenario: returns String(max) when numeric value exceeds criterionMax
whyItMatters: Rubric band inputs must not allow values that exceed the criterion max.
risk: Band max > criterion max makes rubric bands internally inconsistent.

story: clampToCriterionMax returns empty string for empty input
scenario: returns '' when rawValue is an empty string
whyItMatters: Clearing a rubric field must not snap to '0'; empty is intentional.
risk: Auto-filling '0' on clear breaks the user's ability to leave a field blank.

scenario: returns the band's level string when it is a non-empty string
whyItMatters: Rubric band tabs display the level name (e.g., Excellent) as their label.
risk: Blank label causes rubric band tabs to show no text.

story: getBandDisplayLabel fallback when level is empty
scenario: returns 'Band N+1' when band level is an empty string or undefined
whyItMatters: Custom-named bands that are cleared must still show a fallback label.
risk: Blank tab labels make rubric bands indistinguishable from each other.

scenario: returns 'N–M' for a band with valid numeric min and max
whyItMatters: Range label shown in rubric band inspector must accurately reflect the band boundaries.
risk: Wrong separator or NaN produces garbled range labels.

story: getCriterionTintStyle returns style object for valid hex color
scenario: returns {backgroundColor, borderColor, color} for a 6-digit hex string
whyItMatters: Criterion color chips and chart series use this style object.
risk: Missing backgroundColor or wrong alpha makes criterion chips invisible.

story: getCriterionDisplayName uses label, then shortLabel, then fallback
scenario: returns label first; fallback to shortLabel; fallback to 'Criterion N+1'
whyItMatters: Criteria editor row headers must always show a meaningful name.
risk: Empty criterion name displays a blank row header, making the editor unusable.

story: sortMobileJurors sorts alphabetically by name ascending
scenario: name_asc key sorts jurors A–Z case-insensitively
whyItMatters: Mobile heatmap default sort must produce predictable A–Z juror order.
risk: Wrong sort order makes it hard to find a specific juror on mobile.

story: sortMobileJurors sorts alphabetically by name descending
scenario: name_desc key sorts jurors Z–A
whyItMatters: Descending name sort is an explicit user-facing option.
risk: If name_desc returns ascending order, the toggle appears broken.

story: sortMobileJurors sorts by workflow status rank
scenario: status key orders completed < in_progress < not_started; ties broken by name
whyItMatters: Status sort lets admins surface incomplete jurors at the top during a session.
risk: Incorrect rank mapping causes completed jurors to appear at the top, hiding real attention items.

story: sortMobileJurors sorts by average descending, nulls last
scenario: avg_desc key places jurors with higher averages first; nulls sort to bottom
whyItMatters: Top-scorer view helps admins verify high scores are plausible.
risk: Nulls at the top pollute the avg_desc view with incomplete jurors.

story: sortMobileJurors falls back to name_asc for unknown keys
scenario: invalid key string produces A–Z order instead of crashing
whyItMatters: Persisted sort keys may be stale after a refactor; crash would break the heatmap page.
risk: Unhandled key throws TypeError mid-render, leaving the heatmap blank.

story: MOBILE_SORT_KEYS exports the 5 expected option objects
scenario: array has 5 items, each with value and label
whyItMatters: The mobile sort dropdown is built directly from this array; a missing key omits a control.
risk: Wrong count or missing value silently removes sort options from the mobile UI.

story: getPublicAuthFlags resolves with data from RPC
scenario: resolves with the auth flags object when rpc succeeds
whyItMatters: Login page reads these flags to hide disabled auth methods before rendering.
risk: Unresolved promise leaves the login page blank or shows disabled options.

scenario: rejects with the Supabase error object when rpc returns an error
whyItMatters: Callers rely on thrown errors to surface login-page load failures.
risk: Swallowed error silently hides the auth flag load failure.

story: getSecurityPolicy returns the full policy object
scenario: resolves with data when rpc_admin_get_security_policy succeeds
whyItMatters: Security drawer populates fields from this response.
risk: Null response renders security drawer with empty/wrong defaults.

story: setSecurityPolicy calls RPC with the policy wrapped in p_policy
scenario: calls rpc_admin_set_security_policy with { p_policy: policy } and resolves
whyItMatters: Parameter name mismatch silently no-ops the security update.
risk: Wrong param name leaves security settings unchanged without error feedback.

story: setPinPolicy maps params to correct RPC argument names
scenario: calls rpc_admin_set_pin_policy with p_max_attempts, p_cooldown, p_qr_ttl
whyItMatters: Argument-name mismatch causes the DB to use stale PIN policy values.
risk: Wrong param names silently skip the update, leaving old PIN lockout settings in place.

story: listAdminSessions returns rows ordered by last activity
scenario: resolves with data array when from().select().order() succeeds
whyItMatters: Sessions page must list all active admin sessions in recency order.
risk: Wrong return value renders an empty sessions table even when sessions exist.

scenario: rejects with the error when from().select().order() returns an error
whyItMatters: Callers show an error banner when this promise rejects.
risk: Swallowed error hides DB permission failures from the UI.

story: deleteAdminSession resolves when RPC returns ok: true
scenario: resolves with data when rpc_admin_revoke_admin_session returns { ok: true }
whyItMatters: Sessions page removes the session row only when deletion resolves.
risk: Unresolved promise leaves the revoked session in the list.

story: deleteAdminSession throws when RPC returns ok: false
scenario: rejects with the error_code when data.ok === false
whyItMatters: Server-side revocation errors must surface as UI-level error messages.
risk: Silent swallow leaves the session row appearing deleted when it wasn't.

story: touchAdminSession calls invokeEdgeFunction with correct payload
scenario: calls admin-session-touch edge function with normalized payload and resolves on ok: true
whyItMatters: Session touch is called on every admin page load; a bad payload causes silent session tracking failure.
risk: Wrong payload shape causes the edge function to ignore the touch, breaking session analytics.

story: mean computes the arithmetic average of a numeric array
scenario: returns sum/length for non-empty arrays; 0 for empty array
whyItMatters: mean() is the foundation of all analytics averages; wrong result corrupts every chart.
risk: Off-by-one or empty-array crash breaks the analytics tab entirely.

story: stdDev computes population and sample standard deviation
scenario: sample=false → divide by N; sample=true → divide by N-1; < 2 elements → 0
whyItMatters: Juror consistency charts use stdDev; wrong formula shows misleading consistency scores.
risk: Incorrect divisor makes sample stdDev equal population stdDev, hiding small-group variance.

story: quantile interpolates correctly for sorted arrays
scenario: returns median for q=0.5; handles edge-case single-element array
whyItMatters: Boxplot charts depend on q1/q3/median; wrong interpolation shifts whiskers and quartile lines.
risk: Off-by-one in pos calculation produces systematically wrong percentile values.

story: outcomeValues extracts finite numbers from a row array by key
scenario: filters non-finite values (null, NaN, undefined) and converts to Number
whyItMatters: Unfiltered NaN values corrupt mean/stdDev calculations in analytics pipelines.
risk: NaN propagation makes all downstream averages return NaN.

story: fmt1 and fmt2 round to 1 and 2 decimal places respectively
scenario: rounds numeric values correctly; returns null for non-finite input
whyItMatters: Chart labels use fmt1/fmt2 for display; null for non-finite prevents 'NaN' from appearing on charts.
risk: Non-null return for NaN renders 'NaN' in chart axis labels.

story: buildBoxplotStats returns null for empty arrays and correct IQR stats otherwise
scenario: empty → null; non-empty → { q1, med, q3, iqr, whiskerMin, whiskerMax, outliers }
whyItMatters: Boxplot chart requires these exact keys; a null or missing key crashes the chart renderer.
risk: Wrong outlier detection shows non-outliers as extreme values in boxplots.

story: evaluatePassword scores all 5 checks for a strong password
scenario: returns score=5 and all checks=true for a password meeting all criteria
whyItMatters: Password strength indicator drives the security bar and submit-enable logic.
risk: Under-scoring allows weak passwords to pass as strong or prevents valid passwords from submitting.

story: evaluatePassword returns score 0 for empty string and partial scores for partial matches
scenario: empty → score=0; only lowercase → score=1
whyItMatters: Incremental scoring drives the strength bar segments shown during typing.
risk: Non-zero score for empty string shows a non-empty strength bar before any input.

story: isStrongPassword returns true only when all 5 checks pass
scenario: strong password → true; weak password → false
whyItMatters: isStrongPassword gates form submission in the registration and password-change flows.
risk: False positive allows a weak password to pass validation; false negative blocks a valid password.

story: getStrengthMeta returns correct label, color, and pct for each score
scenario: score=5 → 'Very Strong', 100%; score=0 → empty label/color, 0%
whyItMatters: Strength label and bar color are shown in the strength indicator under the password input.
risk: Wrong pct renders the strength bar at the wrong width, giving misleading visual feedback.

scenario: row with written/oral produces UI object with design/delivery keys
whyItMatters: Every score display in the admin and jury UI depends on this mapping; wrong keys show null scores.
risk: Missing key swap leaves design and delivery columns blank in the UI.

story: uiScoresToDb maps design→written and delivery→oral for DB writes
scenario: UI object with design/delivery produces DB row with written/oral keys
whyItMatters: Score saves must write to the correct DB columns or data is stored under wrong criteria.
risk: Wrong column name stores design scores under a null field, silently discarding data.

story: formatMembers normalizes string, array, and null inputs to semicolon-joined string
scenario: null → ''; comma-string → semicolons; array of {name} objects → joined names
whyItMatters: Team member list is displayed from this normalized string; wrong format shows raw JSON in the UI.
risk: Un-normalized array renders '[object Object]' instead of member names.

story: isValidDateParts validates calendar correctness and year bounds
scenario: valid date → true; invalid month, day, or year out of bounds → false
whyItMatters: Admin date inputs (period start/end) use this to prevent invalid date submissions.
risk: Accepting Feb 30 or year 1999 stores invalid dates in the DB.

story: isIsoDateWithinBounds validates ISO string format and range
scenario: valid YYYY-MM-DD within bounds → true; non-string, wrong format, out-of-range → false
whyItMatters: Date filter inputs and period form fields rely on this check before submission.
risk: Accepting non-ISO strings causes Date.parse to return NaN, which propagates silently.

scenario: periods with different start_dates are sorted descending; ties broken by name
whyItMatters: Period selector and period list always show the most recent period at the top.
risk: Wrong order makes admins scroll to find the active period.

story: sortPeriodsForPopover pins selected period and returns recent/all lists
scenario: selectedId match → pinned is that period; rest split into recent (up to 5) and all
whyItMatters: Period selector popover uses this structure to show the current period at the top.
risk: Null pinned when selection exists drops the selected period from the popover header.

story: sortPeriodsForPopover returns null pinned when selectedId has no match
scenario: selectedId='none' → pinned=null; all periods appear in recent/all
whyItMatters: Popover header must gracefully hide when no period is selected.
risk: Non-null pinned for missing ID renders a blank or wrong period in the popover header.

story: stripSlugPrefix removes organization code prefix from period name
scenario: name starting with slug → returns the rest; no match → returns original
whyItMatters: Legacy period names stored with org prefix must be cleaned for display.
risk: Missing strip renders 'boun-cs Spring 2026' instead of 'Spring 2026' in the period selector.

story: validateRubric returns coverageError for empty rubric array
scenario: empty array → coverageError = 'Add at least one band'; no range/level errors
whyItMatters: Criteria editor must prevent save when no rubric bands are defined.
risk: Missing empty-check allows saving criteria with zero rubric bands.

story: validateRubric accepts a valid non-overlapping rubric covering 0–max
scenario: 4 adjacent bands [0–6, 7–15, 16–23, 24–30] → no errors, coverageError=null
whyItMatters: A correctly configured rubric must pass without spurious errors.
risk: False positive forces admins to fix non-existent rubric problems.

scenario: two bands with the same level name → bandLevelErrors for both bands
whyItMatters: Duplicate band names break rubric band selection in the juror eval form.
risk: Two bands named 'Good' make the dropdown indistinguishable.

scenario: two bands whose ranges overlap → bandRangeErrors on both overlapping bands
whyItMatters: Overlapping ranges make rubric scoring ambiguous — a score could map to two bands.
risk: Overlapping bands silently accept scores that should fail validation.

story: validateRubric reports a coverage gap when bands do not fully cover [0–max]
scenario: bands [0–20, 22–30] skip 21 → coverageError mentions gap
whyItMatters: Gaps leave scores in no-man's land — they can't be assigned to any rubric level.
risk: Undetected gap causes undefined behaviour when scoring exactly 21 in a 0–30 rubric.

story: validateCriterion reports errors for missing label, shortLabel, and max
scenario: row with empty label/shortLabel/max → errors.label, errors.shortLabel, errors.max all set
whyItMatters: Criteria save must be blocked when required fields are empty.
risk: Missing field check allows saving incomplete criteria that break analytics.

story: validateCriterion detects duplicate shortLabel across rows
scenario: two rows with shortLabel 'Tech' → errors.shortLabel = 'Duplicate short label' on the second
whyItMatters: Duplicate shortLabels break chart legend disambiguation and report headers.
risk: Two criteria with same shortLabel render overlapping chart series labels.

story: validatePeriodCriteria returns totalError when sum of max values ≠ 100
scenario: two rows with max 60+50=110 → totalError = 'Total must equal 100'
whyItMatters: Criteria weights must sum to 100; any deviation makes analytics percentages wrong.
risk: Missing total check allows saving criteria with weights summing to 110, breaking achievement %.

story: isDisposableEmptyDraftCriterion correctly identifies truly empty draft rows
scenario: fully empty row → true; row with any non-empty field → false
whyItMatters: Save logic strips disposable empty drafts silently; non-empty drafts must be kept.
risk: False positive strips a draft with a partial label, discarding user input.

story: sendEntryTokenEmail calls the correct edge function with recipient payload
scenario: invokes send-entry-token-email with all required fields and returns data
whyItMatters: Entry token emails are the primary way jurors receive their evaluation links.
risk: Wrong edge function name or missing field silently sends a blank email.

story: sendJurorPinEmail calls the correct edge function with PIN payload
scenario: invokes send-juror-pin-email with jurorName, pin, and returns data
whyItMatters: PIN emails are the primary way jurors receive their authentication credentials.
risk: Missing PIN field in the payload means the juror receives an email without their PIN.

story: Notification functions throw when invokeEdgeFunction returns an error
scenario: invokeEdgeFunction returns { error } → function throws instead of returning data
whyItMatters: Email send failures must surface as errors so callers can show failure banners.
risk: Swallowed error makes the admin think the email was sent when it wasn't.

scenario: parseCsv returns array of rows for comma-delimited input
whyItMatters: CSV import relies on parseCsv to split rows and cells.
risk: Wrong parsing would silently corrupt juror/project bulk imports.

scenario: parseCsv handles semicolon-separated CSV
whyItMatters: European spreadsheet exports use semicolon delimiters.
risk: Ignoring semicolons would split no cells, treating each row as one column.

scenario: toNum strips quotes and converts to finite number
whyItMatters: Score values from spreadsheets may be quoted strings.
risk: Non-numeric conversion would set all scores to 0.

scenario: tsToMillis converts dd.mm.yyyy HH:mm to epoch ms
whyItMatters: Score rows stored in EU format must sort correctly.
risk: Wrong parsing returns 0, breaking all timestamp-based sort.

scenario: cmp returns negative/zero/positive for numeric values
whyItMatters: Table sorting depends on cmp for number-aware ordering.
risk: String comparison of numbers produces wrong sort for 10 vs 9.

scenario: rowKey returns jurorId when present
whyItMatters: Row deduplication and edit-map lookups depend on rowKey.
risk: Wrong key causes duplicate rows or missed edits.

scenario: rowKey builds name__dept key when jurorId is absent
whyItMatters: Legacy rows without jurorId must still be keyed uniquely.
risk: Wrong fallback key breaks legacy row lookups.

scenario: adminCompletionPct computes percentage of scored rows
whyItMatters: Progress indicators rely on this percentage.
risk: Wrong % shows wrong progress to admin.

scenario: jurorBg and jurorDot return valid hex strings for a name
whyItMatters: Juror avatar colors must be stable across renders.
risk: Non-hex or empty string breaks avatar CSS.

scenario: dedupeAndSort returns one row per juror+project pair (newest first)
whyItMatters: Score grid deduplication prevents showing stale duplicate scores.
risk: Showing duplicates misleads admin about scoring status.

scenario: AUDIT_PAGE_SIZE equals 50
whyItMatters: Pagination depends on correct page size constant.
risk: Wrong constant sends wrong limit to the RPC.

scenario: parseSearchDateParts('jan 2025') returns {day:null,month:1,year:2025}
whyItMatters: Audit log search uses month-name queries.
risk: Wrong parse returns null, disabling month search.

scenario: parseSearchDateParts('15.06.2025') returns {day:15,month:6,year:2025}
whyItMatters: EU date format is the primary input from Turkish admins.
risk: Wrong parse breaks date-based log search.

scenario: parseSearchDateParts('2025-06-15') returns {day:15,month:6,year:2025}
whyItMatters: ISO dates must also be recognized for international users.
risk: Not recognizing ISO format breaks search for non-TR users.

scenario: parseAuditDateString('2025-06-15') returns {ms, isDateOnly:true}
whyItMatters: Date-only filter adds a full-day window; isDateOnly flag triggers that.
risk: Wrong flag collapses a 24h window to a single ms.

scenario: parseAuditDateString('2025-06-15T14:30') returns {ms, isDateOnly:false}
whyItMatters: Datetime filter must be precise to the minute.
risk: Treating datetime as date-only would extend filter by 24h.

scenario: getAuditDateRangeError returns error string for invalid date
whyItMatters: User sees error guidance when typing an invalid date.
risk: Returning empty string would silently ignore invalid input.

scenario: getAuditDateRangeError returns error when from > to
whyItMatters: Reversed range produces zero results; user needs to know.
risk: No error leaves admin with empty results and no explanation.

scenario: buildAuditParams maps filter fields to RPC param names
whyItMatters: The RPC receives all filters through this function.
risk: Wrong param names silently discard filters.

scenario: getInitials('Ahmet Yıldız') returns 'AY'
whyItMatters: Audit event avatar initials use this function.
risk: Wrong initials corrupt avatar display.

scenario: getActorInfo returns {type:'admin'} for actor_type='admin'
whyItMatters: Audit row actor badge depends on type classification.
risk: Wrong type shows wrong badge icon/color.

scenario: normalizeTeamMemberNames normalizes comma/slash/newline-separated names
whyItMatters: Project bulk import accepts freeform name lists.
risk: Unnormalized names would fail deduplication.

story: groupByDay — groups logs by UTC date
scenario: groupByDay returns day-bucketed array from log array
whyItMatters: Audit log UI renders day separators using groupByDay output.
risk: Wrong grouping shows logs in wrong day bucket.

scenario: formatActionLabel returns human-readable label for known audit action
whyItMatters: Audit log table shows human labels not internal action keys.
risk: Returning raw action key degrades readability.

scenario: formatActionDetail returns juror name from details.juror_name
whyItMatters: Audit drawer shows affected juror name from log details.
risk: Wrong or empty detail loses context for the admin.

scenario: buildProjectMetaMap returns Map keyed by project id with title and students
whyItMatters: Reviews page uses this map to enrich score rows with project metadata.
risk: Wrong key or shape causes missing project titles in score grid.

scenario: buildJurorEditMap stores editEnabled by jurorId and name__dept key
whyItMatters: Edit mode badge on juror rows depends on this map.
risk: Wrong key misses the edit-mode lookup, showing wrong badge state.

scenario: deriveGroupNoOptions deduplicates and sorts group numbers
whyItMatters: Filter dropdown shows unique group options from score rows.
risk: Duplicates in the dropdown confuse admin filter selection.

scenario: applyFilters returns only rows matching the given periodName
whyItMatters: Reviews page is always filtered to a single period.
risk: Rows from other periods leak into the current view.

scenario: applyFilters filters by juror name/affiliation substring
whyItMatters: Juror text search is a primary review-page workflow.
risk: Substring match failing leaves juror filter broken.

scenario: computeActiveFilterCount returns correct count for mixed filter state
whyItMatters: Filter badge shows active filter count on the filter button.
risk: Wrong count shows wrong badge, misleading the admin about active filters.

scenario: listFrameworks queries frameworks table with org OR null filter
whyItMatters: Outcomes page must list org-specific and global frameworks.
risk: Wrong query returns only one type, hiding available frameworks.

scenario: createFramework inserts and returns single record
whyItMatters: Setup wizard creates a framework for the org.
risk: Wrong insert shape fails silently or returns wrong data.

scenario: updateFramework sends update with correct id filter
whyItMatters: Framework rename goes through updateFramework.
risk: Missing .eq() filter would update all frameworks.

scenario: deleteFramework deletes by id and throws on error
whyItMatters: Admin can delete unused frameworks from the outcomes page.
risk: Silent failure leaves orphaned frameworks in the DB.

scenario: createOutcome calls rpc_admin_create_framework_outcome with correct params
whyItMatters: Outcome creation is RPC-based for audit atomicity.
risk: Wrong param names silently skip the audit record.

scenario: cloneFramework calls rpc_admin_clone_framework and returns {id, name}
whyItMatters: Framework clone is used in the setup wizard for standard templates.
risk: Wrong return shape causes the wizard to lose track of the new framework.

scenario: assignFrameworkToPeriod updates periods.framework_id
whyItMatters: Framework assignment drives which outcomes appear in the period.
risk: Wrong update target would assign framework to wrong period.

scenario: listFrameworks throws on supabase error
whyItMatters: Network or RLS errors must surface to the UI.
risk: Swallowing errors leaves the outcomes page in a blank loading state.

scenario: getPlatformSettings calls rpc_admin_get_platform_settings and returns data
whyItMatters: Global settings drawer reads platform config on open.
risk: Wrong RPC name causes drawer to always show default values.

scenario: setPlatformSettings calls rpc with p_platform_name, p_support_email, p_auto_approve_new_orgs
whyItMatters: Platform config must be persisted with exact RPC param names.
risk: Wrong param names silently ignore the save.

scenario: getPlatformSettings throws when RPC returns error
whyItMatters: Super admin must see an error if the config RPC fails.
risk: Swallowing the error leaves drawer in stale state.

scenario: computeCoverage returns correct completed/total ratio; returns '—' when no jurors assigned
whyItMatters: Coverage KPI tells admin how many jurors have completed evaluation.
risk: Wrong count causes misleading completion percentage in reviews page.

scenario: computePending counts unique jurors in ready_to_submit state
whyItMatters: Pending KPI drives urgency messaging for incomplete evaluations.
risk: Counting non-unique jurors inflates the pending number.

scenario: computeSpread returns average inter-juror stddev; excludes projects with <2 completed jurors
whyItMatters: Spread KPI alerts admins to suspicious outlier jurors.
risk: Including single-juror projects biases the spread toward 0.

scenario: Returns count of projects where inter-juror σ exceeds threshold; excludes projects with <2 completed jurors
whyItMatters: High-disagreement KPI highlights projects where evaluators diverge significantly.
risk: Counting single-juror projects or using wrong threshold inflates/deflates the metric.

story: computeOutlierReviews — reviews deviating > threshold from project mean
scenario: Returns count of completed reviews where score deviates more than threshold from that project's mean
whyItMatters: Outlier KPI flags individual reviews that may need admin attention.
risk: Including incomplete reviews or wrong mean calculation produces false positives.

scenario: getCellState returns empty for null entry, partial when some criteria filled, scored when all filled
whyItMatters: Cell state drives heatmap coloring and juror workflow transitions.
risk: Wrong state causes juror to appear complete when only partially scored.

scenario: getPartialTotal sums only filled numeric criteria fields
whyItMatters: Partial total shows progress score when juror hasn't finished all criteria.
risk: Including null/string fields corrupts the partial total.

scenario: getJurorWorkflowState correctly transitions: editing > completed > ready_to_submit > in_progress > not_started
whyItMatters: Workflow state determines juror badge and export status.
risk: Wrong state exposes submit button to incomplete jurors.

scenario: getProjectHighlight returns outstanding/consistent/high/strong/fallback based on score distribution
whyItMatters: Highlight phrase appears in rankings and overview for each top project.
risk: Wrong phrase breaks the rule order and shows lower-priority phrase.

scenario: computeOverviewMetrics counts completed/editing/in_progress/not_started/ready_to_submit jurors correctly
whyItMatters: Overview KPI cards read from computeOverviewMetrics; wrong counts mislead admin.
risk: Off-by-one in status logic hides incomplete jurors in the overview.

scenario: scoreBgColor and scoreCellStyle return null for no score; return rgba string for valid score
whyItMatters: Score color palette drives heatmap cell aesthetics.
risk: Null return on valid score leaves cell uncolored.

scenario: buildLookup builds jurorKey → projectId → entry map including criteria fields
whyItMatters: buildLookup is the central data structure for the heatmap grid.
risk: Wrong key mapping causes cell data to appear under wrong juror/project.

scenario: buildJurorFinalMap returns Map<jurorKey, boolean> for final submission status
whyItMatters: Final map drives completed state in the heatmap grid.
risk: Missing entries leave juror marked as not-final when they are.

scenario: filterCompletedJurors excludes jurors in edit mode even if finalSubmitted is true
whyItMatters: Average row must not include jurors currently editing their scores.
risk: Including editing jurors skews the average row values.

scenario: computeGroupAverages returns 2-decimal average for groups with ≥1 scored juror; null otherwise
whyItMatters: Average column is the primary benchmark row in the heatmap.
risk: Including partial cells in the average distorts benchmark scores.

scenario: buildExportRowsData maps juror scores per group with null for unscored cells
whyItMatters: Export rows are the source for the Excel/CSV export.
risk: Wrong null handling writes 0 instead of blank in the export.

scenario: when activeTab is a criterion id, scores contain that criterion's value instead of total
whyItMatters: CSV/PDF export must reflect the selected criterion tab, not always the all-criteria total.
risk: Without this, selecting 'Technical Content' tab still exports totals — the export is misleading.

story: computeGroupAverages — juror absent from lookup, partial scores excluded
scenario: juror with no lookup entry contributes nothing; juror with partial cell state is excluded; only 'scored' entries drive the average
whyItMatters: Averages are shown in the heatmap average row — including partial or absent data would inflate/corrupt displayed scores.
risk: A missing lookup key causing a crash or a partial score counted as a total would silently skew averages.

story: buildExportRowsData — empty groups and absent-juror lookup edge cases
scenario: empty groups array returns rows with empty scores objects; juror completely absent from lookup produces all-null scores without crashing
whyItMatters: Export must never crash on edge data — empty periods or newly added jurors must produce clean rows.
risk: Accessing undefined lookup entry without optional chaining would throw a TypeError and block the export.

scenario: parseProjectsCsv detects header row by alias and maps title/members columns
whyItMatters: Bulk project import must work with any column naming convention.
risk: Header mis-detection causes all data rows to be treated as headers.

scenario: parseProjectsCsv marks rows as duplicate when title matches existing projects or appears twice in file
whyItMatters: Duplicate detection prevents importing the same project twice.
risk: Missing duplicate check would create phantom duplicate projects.

scenario: parseProjectsCsv marks rows with missing title as error; warningMessage summarizes issues
whyItMatters: Admin must see clear feedback before confirming an import with errors.
risk: Silent error rows would import projects without titles.

scenario: parseJurorsCsv maps juror_name / affiliation / email from header or positional columns
whyItMatters: Bulk juror import populates the jury list for a period.
risk: Wrong column mapping imports affiliations as names.

scenario: parseJurorsCsv marks rows duplicate when name matches existing jurors; marks error when name missing
whyItMatters: Prevents importing duplicate jurors and blank-name records.
risk: Missing validation would create jurors without names.

scenario: formatDiffChips returns diff entries for criteria.save changes, periods.update changedFields, and trigger-based before/after diffs
whyItMatters: Audit diff chips visualise what changed in each event — wrong data would mislead admins reviewing configuration changes.
risk: Incorrect field mapping would show wrong before/after values or skip changed fields.

story: groupBulkEvents — singles pass through, burst collapses to bulk
scenario: groupBulkEvents wraps isolated events as type='single'; collapses 3+ same-actor/resource events within 5min into type='bulk'
whyItMatters: Audit timeline readability depends on correct bulk grouping — wrong grouping hides individual events or creates false bulk items.
risk: Off-by-one in window check would fail to collapse bursts or collapse unrelated events.

story: formatEventMeta — bulk count, export format, diff chip, IP
scenario: formatEventMeta builds monospace metadata line: bulk shows 'action × N'; export shows format + rowCount; diff chip appends 'key from→to'; fallback appends IP
whyItMatters: Meta line is the secondary description in the audit table — wrong output degrades readability and trust.
risk: Branch ordering bug could show IP when bulk count is present, or skip export format.

scenario: addDaySeparators inserts type='day' items between groups from different calendar days; empty input returns []
whyItMatters: Day separators in the audit timeline give temporal context — missing separators merge days visually.
risk: Wrong date key comparison would interleave separators within the same day.

scenario: detectAnomalies returns login-failure anomaly for ≥3 failures in 24h; org-suspension anomaly; PIN-lockout anomaly; returns null when no anomalies
whyItMatters: Anomaly detection surfaces potential security incidents — missing detection leaves admins unaware of attacks.
risk: Wrong time-window comparison would miss real bursts or false-trigger on old events.

story: computeNeedsAttention — stale jurors and incomplete projects
scenario: computeNeedsAttention flags jurors with progress=0 or status='not_started' and projects with completedEvals < totalJurors
whyItMatters: Overview attention cards guide admins to act before evaluation closes — wrong filtering shows wrong items.
risk: Off-by-one on completedEvals comparison would hide projects that still need more evaluations.

story: computeTopProjects — top N sorted by totalAvg, requires ≥5 projects
scenario: computeTopProjects returns [] when fewer than 5 projects; for ≥5 projects returns top limit sorted by totalAvg desc with rank field
whyItMatters: Overview ranking card motivates teams — wrong sorting or wrong threshold shows trivial or incorrect rankings.
risk: Filter excluding null totalAvg could silently reduce ranking below limit.

scenario: when totalJurors=0 no projects are incomplete; juror with status=not_started is stale even if progress>0; empty arrays behave like null
whyItMatters: Attention card must not show false positives when no jurors are assigned, and must catch jurors who reset progress but kept a stale status flag.
risk: totalJurors=0 with completed<0 comparisons could show all projects as incomplete; OR condition bug would let not_started jurors go undetected.

story: computeTopProjects — tie at 3rd position and all-null totalAvg
scenario: when two projects tie at rank 3 the one appearing earlier in the input array wins (stable sort); when all projects have null totalAvg the result is []
whyItMatters: Tie-breaking must be deterministic — an unstable sort would flip rankings between renders and confuse faculty.
risk: Using unstable sort would make rank 3 non-deterministic; missing null-filter guard could cause NaN in the ranking card.

scenario: Array has 5 columns with correct keys/labels/sortKeys; each getValue transforms a log row to the expected display value; getChipLabel maps known resource_types and falls back for unknown/undefined; action column falls back to r.action when formatSentence returns null
whyItMatters: Audit log table columns drive every cell displayed to admins — a wrong getValue silently shows incorrect data for every row.
risk: CHIP_MAP miss for a new resource_type returns raw DB string instead of a user-friendly chip label.

story: getJurorById — fetches a single juror row by id
scenario: getJurorById calls supabase.from('jurors').select('*').eq('id', jurorId).single(); returns data on success and throws on error
whyItMatters: Jury identity step relies on this to verify the juror exists in the period.
risk: A wrong table name or missing .single() would silently return an array instead of one object.

story: getJurorEditState — maps edit_enabled + expiry timestamp to edit_allowed boolean
scenario: Returns edit_allowed=true when edit_enabled=true and edit_expires_at is in the future; returns false when expired or disabled; maps is_blocked to lock_active
whyItMatters: Jury evaluate page uses this to decide whether to show the score form or a locked state — wrong mapping locks out active jurors.
risk: Date.parse on an ISO string vs Date.now() comparison is order-sensitive — off-by-one would allow expired sessions.

scenario: finalizeJurorSubmission passes p_period_id, p_juror_id, p_session_token, and a random p_correlation_id; returns data on success and throws on RPC error
whyItMatters: Finalizing a submission is irreversible from the juror's perspective — an uncaught error here would silently drop the finalization.
risk: randomUUID() injection pattern is critical; hardcoded UUID would make two submissions indistinguishable in audit logs.

story: freezePeriodSnapshot — passes p_force only when force=true
scenario: Without force flag: only p_period_id sent. With force=true: p_force included. RPC error is thrown.
whyItMatters: Freezing a snapshot is a one-way admin action — accidentally including p_force on a non-force call could re-freeze an already valid snapshot.
risk: Conditional param inclusion means a default-true force flag would permanently change semantics.

story: getProjectRankings — returns [] when data is null
scenario: getProjectRankings calls rpc_jury_project_rankings with period_id and session_token; null data response returns []; non-null data returned directly
whyItMatters: Rankings page renders from this array — a null return would crash the map.
risk: Forgetting the || [] guard would surface as a runtime crash only on periods with no scores.

story: submitJuryFeedback — passes null comment and correct params
scenario: submitJuryFeedback passes p_period_id, p_session_token, p_rating, and p_comment (null when falsy); throws on RPC error
whyItMatters: Jury feedback is the final step after scoring — a wrong comment coercion (undefined instead of null) could fail the RPC NOT NULL constraint.
risk: Passing undefined vs null to a PostgreSQL RPC column with NOT NULL raises a DB error.

story: computeSetupPercent + computeRingModel — lifecycle state to ring model
scenario: computeSetupPercent returns null for missing readiness, 100 for ok:true, and partial% for required issues; computeRingModel maps 5 lifecycle states to percent/label/stateClass
whyItMatters: The setup ring is the primary visual indicator of period readiness — a wrong percent makes it look done when it isn't.
risk: computeSetupPercent divides by SETUP_REQUIRED_TOTAL — if that constant drifts from the RPC check count, % goes above 100.

story: Juror helper functions — color, class, score style, edit window
scenario: groupBarColor and groupTextClass return correct tokens for none/partial/done; mobileScoreStyle returns correct colors for score ranges; formatEditWindowLeft formats time remaining; isEditWindowActive returns false for past timestamps
whyItMatters: Juror table cells use these to color-code evaluation progress — a wrong return shows all rows in the wrong state.
risk: groupBarColor checks scored >= total (not ===), so fractional over-scoring could misclassify a row.

story: Project helper functions — membersToArray, membersToString, scoreBandToken, getProjectCell
scenario: membersToArray handles null/array/string inputs; membersToString joins with ', '; scoreBandToken returns correct CSS var token for high/mid/low scores; getProjectCell returns correct value for each key
whyItMatters: Member display and score banding are shown on every project row — wrong parsing silently drops team member names.
risk: membersToArray split regex covers comma/semicolon/newline — a missed delimiter leaves the raw string as one entry.

story: Outcome helper functions — coverageBadgeClass, coverageLabel, naturalCodeSort
scenario: coverageBadgeClass returns correct CSS class for direct/indirect/other; coverageLabel returns correct label; naturalCodeSort sorts codes numerically handling multi-level codes and (copy) suffix
whyItMatters: Outcomes table uses these to display coverage type and sort order — a wrong badge class shows wrong visual state for every outcome.
risk: naturalCodeSort strips leading letters before comparing — if a code has no numeric part, NaN comparison silently mis-sorts.

story: parseUserAgent identifies browser from UA string
scenario: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Opera, Safari, Unknown each detected correctly from real UA strings
whyItMatters: Browser field appears in audit log; wrong detection misattributes sessions.
risk: OPR/Edge UA string order matters — wrong priority causes Chrome/Edge to match first.

story: parseUserAgent identifies OS from UA string
scenario: Windows, Android, iOS, macOS, Linux, Unknown each detected correctly
whyItMatters: OS field appears in audit log session records.
risk: Android before iPhone check; Linux after Mac — wrong order causes false positives.

story: maskIpAddress redacts IPv4 and IPv6 addresses
scenario: IPv4 last octet replaced with xxx; IPv6 last groups replaced; empty/invalid returns Unknown
whyItMatters: IP masking is a privacy requirement before storing in audit log.
risk: IPv6 colon splitting may leave too few segments — boundary case returns Unknown instead of masked form.

story: normalizeCountryCode validates and uppercases 2-letter ISO codes
scenario: Valid 2-char codes returned uppercase; empty/invalid/non-alpha2 return Unknown
whyItMatters: Country code appears in audit log geolocation; invalid codes break country flag rendering.
risk: Regex /^[A-Z]{2}$/ run after toUpperCase() — input casing does not affect result.

scenario: email/google/github providers mapped to labels; multiple providers joined with +; fallback to Unknown
whyItMatters: Auth method shown in admin audit log session panel.
risk: providers array takes precedence over single provider — if both present, array wins.

story: getAdminDeviceId returns a stable device identifier
scenario: Returns a string prefixed dev_; caches in memory on second call without hitting localStorage
whyItMatters: Device ID is sent with every admin RPC call for session tracking.
risk: Module-level in-memory cache means id is stable within a test run but not across module reloads.

story: emit adds a toast and notifies subscribers
scenario: emit() returns incremented id, listeners receive updated list, toast fields set correctly
whyItMatters: All user-facing success/error messages go through toastStore.emit.
risk: id increments module-globally — tests that share module state may see unexpected ids.

scenario: Subscriber receives updates; unsubscribe function removes it from future notifications
